input.final_summary: Mapped org-next's TanStack Start setup, workspace integration, route generation, static analysis config, ports, and app-specific auth/session references. Key takeaway: an empty virtual-terminal app can mirror the TanStack shell and workspace scripts, but should choose its own port/app slug and avoid org-next auth, tenant, RMP, Rollbar, and domain assumptions unless intentionally adopting them.

content.text:
Here’s the concise map for mirroring `apps/org-next` into a new empty `apps/virtual-terminal` TanStack Start app.

## Relevant Files
- `/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/package.json`: app name, scripts, TanStack/Vite/React deps, `fallow` script.
- `/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/tsconfig.json`: local TS config with `@/* -> ./src/*`, strict settings, Vite/Vitest/Node types.
- `/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/vite.config.ts`: TanStack Start plugin, Nitro, React, Tailwind, alias, test config, dev server port `3210`.
- `/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/src/router.tsx`: `createRouter`, `routeTree`, React Query SSR integration.
- `/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/src/start.ts`: TanStack Start instance and request middleware.
- `/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/src/routes/__root.tsx`: root route shell, document layout, CSS import, title, devtools.
- `/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/src/routes/_authed/index.tsx` or a simpler route: model for first file route.
- `/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/src/routeTree.gen.ts`: generated by TanStack Router, imported by `router.tsx`, not hand-edited.
- `/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/biome.jsonc`: app-level Biome config extending root and excluding `routeTree.gen.ts`.
- `/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/.fallowrc.json`: app-level Fallow boundaries, entry points, ignores, duplicate mode.
- `/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/playwright.config.ts`: e2e server setup and port `3210`.
- `/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/.gitignore`: ignores `.tanstack`, `.output`, `.fallow`, Playwright output, etc.
- `/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/components.json`: shadcn/UI alias config, only needed if the new app uses that pattern.
- `/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/src/styles.css`, `/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/src/vite-env.d.ts`, `/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/public/robots.txt`: likely baseline assets.

## Root Integration
- `/Users/duhl/git/ui/pnpm-workspace.yaml` already includes `apps/*`, so `apps/virtual-terminal` would be picked up automatically.
- `/Users/duhl/git/ui/turbo.json` defines generic `build`, `dev`, `test`, `e2e`, `typecheck`, and `fallow` tasks; adding matching package scripts makes Turbo discover them.
- `/Users/duhl/git/ui/package.json` root scripts use Turbo and Biome globally; `pnpm run validate` will include the new app if scripts exist.
- `/Users/duhl/git/ui/biome.jsonc` globally excludes `**/routeTree.gen.ts`.
- `/Users/duhl/git/ui/Caddyfile` has no `org-next`/`3210` proxy; `org.anedot.me` points to legacy `3203`, auth to `3208`. A new app needs explicit proxy wiring only if it should run behind local Caddy.

## Implementation Considerations
- Use a unique package name, probably `"virtual-terminal"`, and choose a unique dev/e2e port. `org-next` hardcodes `3210` in `package.json`, `vite.config.ts`, `playwright.config.ts`, docs, and auth integration tests.
- For an empty app, start leaner than `org-next`: keep TanStack Start, Router, React, Vite, Tailwind, Nitro, TypeScript, Biome, Vitest. Avoid copying `rollbar`, RMP/MSW, tenant/session/campaign/org API deps unless needed.
- Keep `src/routeTree.gen.ts` generated. The route filename convention matters: periods become path separators, e.g. `test-seed.auth.ts` -> `/test-seed/auth`.
- Avoid org-next-specific references: `ORG_NEXT_APP_SLUG`, `APP_SLUG = "org-next"`, `VITE_AUTH_APP_URL` flows, `AUTH_APPS["org-next"]`, cookie/session comments, title `"Org Next"`, Rollbar source `"org-next-client"`, tenant bootstrap, `_authed` org flows, and `ANEDOT_API_URL_BASE` assumptions.
- If `virtual-terminal` integrates with the auth app, it needs its own app slug and `AUTH_APPS` entry, allowed origin/port, and shared session secret env. Do not reuse `ORG_NEXT_SESSION_SECRET` naming except as a pattern.
- Fallow boundaries can be copied structurally, but adjust zones to actual folders. For a minimal empty app, `entry: ["src/**/*"]`, `ignorePatterns` for generated/config/test files, and `publicPackages` matching workspace packages are the main pieces.
